title: Implement type mapping and enum support for ToolArgs derive
purpose: Extend the ToolArgs derive macro to handle all Rust types that appear in tool args structs: integers, floats, Option<T>, Vec<T>, HashMap<String, T>, serde_json::Value, nested structs, and enums (string, tagged, untagged, adjacently tagged). After this brief, the macro can generate correct JSON Schema for every type used across the existing 17+ tools.
task: Add type-to-JSON-Schema mapping logic in tool_args/schema.rs and create tool_args/enum_schema.rs for enum handling. The type mapping is closed — unsupported types produce compile_error! with a message suggesting #[tool_args(schema = {...})]. Enum support covers four serde representations: unit-variant-only (string enum), internally tagged, externally tagged/untagged (oneOf), and adjacently tagged. Nested structs with #[derive(ToolArgs)] generate inline object schemas via recursive json_schema() calls.

requirements.title: Integer type mapping
requirements.spec: Signed integer types (i8, i16, i32, i64, isize) SHALL generate {"type": "integer"}. Unsigned integer types (u8, u16, u32, u64, usize) SHALL generate {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0}.
acceptance: A field of type i64 generates {"type": "integer"} with no minimum
A field of type u64 generates {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0}
A field of type u32 generates {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0}
All signed types (i8, i16, i32, i64, isize) produce identical schema
All unsigned types (u8, u16, u32, u64, usize) produce identical schema with minimum: 0

requirements.title: Float type mapping
acceptance: A field of type f64 generates {"type": "number"}
A field of type f32 generates {"type": "number"}

requirements.title: Option<T> handling
requirements.spec: Fields of type Option<T> SHALL generate the inner type T's schema and SHALL omit the field from the required array. Option<T> SHALL NOT produce a nullable schema — absent means None.
acceptance: A field offset: Option<u64> generates {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0} and is absent from required
A field name: Option<String> generates {"type": "string"} and is absent from required
Option<Option<T>> produces the same schema as Option<T> (double-unwrap)

requirements.title: Vec<T> and HashMap<String, T> mapping
requirements.spec: Vec<T> fields SHALL generate {"type": "array", "items": <schema of T>}. HashMap<String, T> fields SHALL generate {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": <schema of T>}. HashMap keys that are not String SHALL produce compile_error!.
acceptance: A field tags: Vec<String> generates {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}
A field counts: Vec<u64> generates {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0}}
A field metadata: HashMap<String, String> generates {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": {"type": "string"}}
A field with HashMap<i32, String> produces a compile error mentioning String key requirement

requirements.title: serde_json::Value escape hatch
requirements.spec: Fields of type serde_json::Value (or Value if imported) SHALL generate {} (empty schema, accepting any JSON value).
acceptance: A field content: serde_json::Value generates {} (empty object schema)
A field content: Value generates {} when Value resolves to serde_json::Value
The field still appears in required unless #[serde(default)] is present

requirements.title: Nested struct support
requirements.spec: Fields whose type is a struct with #[derive(ToolArgs)] SHALL generate an inline object schema by calling T::json_schema() in the generated code. The generated code SHALL emit T::json_schema() as a function call, not attempt to introspect the nested struct at macro expansion time.
acceptance: A struct Inner with #[derive(ToolArgs)] used as a field type in Outer generates Inner::json_schema() call in the expanded code
The resulting schema nests Inner's properties inside Outer's properties
Nested structs respect their own required/additionalProperties rules
cargo check succeeds when both Outer and Inner have #[derive(ToolArgs)]

requirements.title: Unsupported type compile error
requirements.spec: IF a field's type does not match any entry in the type mapping table AND the field has no #[tool_args(schema = {...})] override, THEN the macro SHALL emit compile_error! with a message naming the unsupported type and suggesting the override syntax.
acceptance: A field of type PathBuf produces compile_error!("ToolArgs: unsupported type `PathBuf`. Use #[tool_args(schema = {...})] to provide a manual schema.")
The error span points to the field, not the derive invocation
The error message includes the exact type name from the source

requirements.title: String enum schema generation
requirements.spec: WHEN ToolArgs is derived on an enum with only unit variants, the macro SHALL generate {"type": "string", "enum": [<variant names>]}. Variant names SHALL respect #[serde(rename_all)] on the enum and #[serde(rename)] on individual variants. Doc comments on variants SHALL be collected into a top-level description listing each option.
acceptance: An enum with variants A, B, C generates {"type": "string", "enum": ["A", "B", "C"]}
An enum with #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] and variants MyVariant generates enum: ["my_variant"]
An enum with #[serde(rename = "custom")] on a variant uses "custom" in the enum array
Doc comments on variants produce a description like "A: docs for A. B: docs for B."

requirements.title: Tagged and untagged enum schema generation
requirements.spec: WHEN ToolArgs is derived on an enum with #[serde(tag = "...")], the macro SHALL generate a oneOf schema where each variant is an object with the tag field as a const value. WHEN #[serde(untagged)] is used, the macro SHALL generate oneOf without a discriminator. WHEN #[serde(tag = "t", content = "c")] is used (adjacent tagging), the macro SHALL generate a two-field object per variant with the tag as const and the content wrapping the variant data.
acceptance: A tagged enum with #[serde(tag = "type")] and variants Create{title: String} and Update{id: String} generates oneOf with two objects, each having a const type field
An untagged enum generates oneOf without any discriminator const fields
An adjacently tagged enum with tag = "t", content = "c" generates objects with t as const and c wrapping the variant data
Enum variants with named fields generate object schemas with properties matching the field names

requirements.title: Integration tests for type mapping and enums
requirements.spec: Integration tests SHALL verify correct schema generation for all type mappings and enum representations. Tests SHALL cover: integer types (signed and unsigned), floats, Option<T>, Vec<T>, HashMap<String, T>, serde_json::Value, nested structs, unsupported types (compile-fail), string enums, tagged enums, and untagged enums.
acceptance: At least 12 new test cases added to crates/norn-macros/tests/tool_args.rs
Test: i64 field → integer schema
Test: u32 field → integer schema with minimum 0
Test: f64 field → number schema
Test: Option<String> field → string schema, absent from required
Test: Vec<String> field → array schema with string items
Test: HashMap<String, i64> field → object schema with integer additionalProperties
Test: serde_json::Value field → empty schema {}
Test: nested #[derive(ToolArgs)] struct → inline object schema
Test: string enum with rename_all → correct enum values
Test: tagged enum → oneOf with discriminator
All tests pass with cargo test -p norn-macros

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement #[serde(rename)] or #[serde(rename_all)] on struct fields — field-level rename is NTM-003 (enum variant rename IS in scope for this brief since it's part of enum schema generation)
SHALL NOT implement #[serde(flatten)] — that is NTM-003
SHALL NOT implement #[tool_args(...)] override attributes — those are NTM-003
SHALL NOT implement compile-time doc comment enforcement — that is NTM-003
SHALL NOT modify any existing tool implementations — migration is NTM-005
verification: cargo check -p norn-macros succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn-macros -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo test -p norn-macros passes all tests including new type mapping and enum tests
A struct using every supported type (String, bool, i64, u64, f64, Option<String>, Vec<String>, HashMap<String, String>, serde_json::Value) compiles and produces a valid schema
An enum with #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] unit variants produces correct enum values
A tagged enum produces oneOf with discriminator fields
An unsupported type (e.g., PathBuf) produces a compile error with a helpful message
